ddls:
CREATE TABLE "documents" (
  "doc_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each document */,
  "doc_title" TEXT /* Title of the document */,
  "doc_type" TEXT /* Type of the document (e.g., Hardware Design, Firmware Update) */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the document was created */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date the document was last updated */,
  "current_version" TEXT /* Current version of the document */,
  "author_id" INTEGER /* ID of the author who created the document */,
  "parent_doc_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent document (if any) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the document (e.g., draft, approved, archived) */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the document (e.g., hardware, firmware, user manual) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("doc_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_documents_author_id FOREIGN KEY ("author_id") REFERENCES authors ("author_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "document_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "doc_id" INTEGER /* ID of the document this version belongs to */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number of the document */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date the version was created */,
  "changes_made" TEXT /* Description of changes made in this version */,
  "author_id" INTEGER /* ID of the author who made the changes */,
  "rollback_version" TEXT /* Version to roll back to (if any) */,
  "comparison_version" TEXT /* Version to compare with (if any) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_document_versions_author_id FOREIGN KEY ("author_id") REFERENCES authors ("author_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_document_versions_doc_id FOREIGN KEY ("doc_id") REFERENCES documents ("doc_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "authors" (
  "author_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each author */,
  "author_name" TEXT /* Full name of the author */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the author */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the author belongs to */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the author (e.g., engineer, technical writer) */,
  "team" TEXT /* Team the author belongs to (e.g., hardware, firmware) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("author_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "document_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "doc_id" INTEGER /* ID of the document being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the document */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the document was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the document */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_document_access_doc_id FOREIGN KEY ("doc_id") REFERENCES documents ("doc_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_document_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, manager) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "teams" (
  "team_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each team */,
  "team_name" TEXT /* Name of the team */,
  "team_lead" INTEGER /* ID of the team lead */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("team_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_teams_team_lead FOREIGN KEY ("team_lead") REFERENCES authors ("author_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "document_teams" (
  "doc_id" INTEGER /* ID of the document */,
  "team_id" INTEGER /* ID of the team associated with the document */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("doc_id", "team_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "document_categories" (
  "doc_id" INTEGER /* ID of the document */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category associated with the document */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("doc_id", "category")
)
column_names: Type of the document (e.g., Hardware Design, Firmware Update)
